title: centaurea carduiformis subsp. carduiformis
intro: Centaurea carduiformis subsp. carduiformis, also known as the Great Yellow Thistle, is a plant species that belongs to the Asteraceae family and is native to the mountainous regions of Central Asia. This plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to reduce inflammation and its antiseptic and antibacterial properties, making it effective against wounds and infections. The health benefits of Centaurea carduiformis subsp. carduiformis include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and even show promise in treating certain types of cancer. The plant is typically prepared as a herbal tea or infused oil, and it can also be used in tinctures and ointments to harness its medicinal properties.
